{"label": "CoT/simple", "confidence": 0.9}

Authentication and authorization are two fundamental concepts in security, but they serve different purposes.

Authentication is the process of verifying the identity of a user or system. It ensures that the entity trying to access a system or resource is who they claim to be. For example, when you log in to your email account, the service checks your username and password against its records to authenticate your identity.

Authorization, on the other

```sql
SELECT c.id, c.name, c.email, COUNT(o.id) AS order_count, SUM(o.amount) AS total_spend
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id
WHERE o.created_at > CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'
GROUP BY c.id
HAVING COUNT(o.id) > 3;
```

```json
{"label": "code/simple", "confidence": 1.0}
```
